content.problemStatement:
Rupsa recently started to intern under Chef. He gave her **N** type of ingredients of varying quantity ** A1, A2, ..., AN** respectively to store it. But as she is lazy to arrange them she puts them all in a storage box.

Chef comes up with a new recipe and decides to prepare it. He asks Rupsa to get **two** units of each type ingredient for the dish. But when she went to retrieve the ingredients, she realizes that she can only pick one item at a time from the box and can know its type only after she has picked it out. The picked item is not put back in the bag.

She, being lazy, wants to know the maximum number of times she would need to pick items from the box in the worst case so that it is guaranteed that she gets at least two units of each type of ingredient. If it is impossible to pick items in such a way, print -1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N on the first line, followed by N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN on the second line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum number of times Rupsa would need to pick items from the box in the worst case so that it is guaranteed that she gets at least two units of each type of ingredient. If it is impossible to pick items in such a way, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, Rupsa needs to pick 4 items to guarantee that she has at least two of each ingredient. In the second test case, she only needs to pick 2 items because there is only one type of ingredient.
In this test case, Rupsa cannot guarantee that she has at least two of each ingredient because the maximum quantity of any ingredient is 3, which is greater than the number of ingredients.
In this test case, Rupsa needs to pick 12 items to guarantee that she has at least two of the only ingredient.

solution.approach: The key insight is to find the maximum quantity of any ingredient and calculate the worst-case scenario. If the maximum quantity is 1, it is impossible to pick two units of each type of ingredient. Otherwise, the worst-case scenario is when Rupsa picks all the ingredients of the type with the maximum quantity first, and then picks the remaining ingredients.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — no additional space is used.
